claim: The single fact that a home-prefixed chunk carrying printable text is no longer isPureControl (isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text') === false / isRepaintChunk(...) === true) is separately pinned with near-duplicate vectors and near-identical narrative comments in three files: tests/main/needs-input.test.ts (TEST-2527), tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts (TEST-2524), and tests/characterization-status.test.ts (TEST-2526).
fix: Consolidate: let characterization-status.test.ts's CHAR-001 keep its narrow historical amendment pin (its distinct change-detector role per CLAUDE.md), and fold TEST-2527's overlapping vectors into needs-input-classifier.test.ts's exhaustive REQ-009 enumeration instead of keeping a third near-duplicate assertion set in needs-input.test.ts, to cut the number of places a future change to this classification rule must be updated in lockstep.
resolution: Deferred by explicit human decision recorded in ESC-001 (state.json escalations[0].decision, 2026-07-09: '...defer 001/002/003 to followups'), following the same-round precedent already applied to FINDING-011/FINDING-012. Deferral record verified ADEQUATE by reading docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md LOW section 'Triple-pinned classification vector (FINDING-001, quality)' (lines 53-60): it restates the claim (the single isPureControl/isRepaintChunk fact pinned in three files -- TEST-2527, TEST-2524, TEST-2526), names the reason for deferral (all three suites are frozen; consolidation is a tests-phase refactor), and gives the concrete follow-up path (keep CHAR-001's change-detector role, fold TEST-2527's overlap into the classifier suite) -- matching the fix text's own recommendation. Confirmed unfixed in the current tree (all three pins still present, as expected for a deferred item) -- this is a deferral closure, not a code fix.

claim: tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts (TEST-2541) asserts REQ-008's regression nets survive by grep-matching the literal it(...) description strings of tests in tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts and tests/e2e/marker-less-pane.spec.ts, rather than referencing a shared stable identifier. This couples a separate guard file to prose text with no compiler/test-runner enforced link: a purely cosmetic rewording of either pinned it() description (no behavior change) silently breaks this guard, while the guard provides no protection against the pinned assertion bodies being weakened in place (only the description string is checked).
fix: If a presence-guard is wanted for tests outside the gate profile, anchor it to something less prose-coupled -- e.g. grep for the stable TEST-QA11 id already used in the e2e spec (as TEST-2541 already does for that one), or a similar stable id prefix in the unit pins -- instead of the full free-text it() description, so a non-behavioral rename doesn't force an unrelated file edit.
resolution: Deferred by explicit human decision recorded in ESC-001 (state.json escalations[0].decision, 2026-07-09: '...defer 001/002/003 to followups'), following the same-round precedent already applied to FINDING-011/FINDING-012. Deferral record verified ADEQUATE by reading docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md LOW section 'Prose-coupled regression-net guard (FINDING-002, quality)' (lines 62-67): it restates the claim (tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts / TEST-2541 grep-matches literal it() description strings instead of a stable id), names the reason for deferral (the guard still catches deletion, its actual purpose; anchoring on stable TEST-ids in both directions is a tests-phase cleanup for the next status feature). Re-read tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts directly: it still asserts via toContain() on the literal it(...) description strings, confirming the finding is genuinely deferred (unfixed), not silently addressed -- this is a deferral closure, not a code fix.

claim: Repaint classification is a pure function of TRANSPORT CHUNK FRAMING, which is nondeterministic over the ssh/remote-agent transports the feature targets: a repaint whose CURSOR_HOME_RE prefix is split across pty-read/TCP boundaries has its continuation fragment (printable screen text, or a truncated-escape remnant - isPureControl('\x1b[') is false, so '[' is admitted as printable) classified as REAL output, restarting the quiet window, resetting needs-input->busy, and marking a marker-less pane busy - the same underlying byte stream yields different tracker states depending on framing. This is a pre-existing baseline property (split delivery classified identically pre-0025, and 0025 actually narrows the divergence: whole-chunk went from wedged-busy to needs-input), but the spec vocabulary and all 26 vectors assume whole-chunk delivery, and the load-bearing comment 'A full-screen ConPTY repaint arrives as one big chunk' (status-tracker.ts:62) is an empirical local-ConPTY claim, not an ssh guarantee. No test or living doc characterizes the split-delivery degradation.
fix: Add a characterization vector to tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts: the REQ-001 repaint bytes delivered as two chunks (split after the home prefix) -> continuation classifies as real output, degradation is graceful (prompt still admitted, needs-input fires one quietMs later, no wedge); add one sentence to docs/features/status-engine.md stating classification is per-chunk and framing-dependent, degrading to real-output semantics on split delivery. propose CONV: A per-chunk stream classifier whose input framing is transport-dependent MUST document and pin (via a split-delivery characterization test) its behavior when a classified unit spans a chunk boundary.
resolution: Deferred to docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md per explicit human decision recorded in ESC-001 (2026-07-09: 'defer 001/002/003 to followups'), following the FINDING-011/012 deferral-resolution precedent. Deferral record verified ADEQUATE by reading the ledger (LOW section, 'Chunk-framing sensitivity of repaint classification'): it states the defect accurately (per-chunk classification; a CURSOR_HOME_RE prefix split across pty-read/TCP boundaries classifies the continuation as real output), records that 0025 narrows rather than widens the divergence, and names the revisit path (a cross-chunk escape reassembler, OSC-133 split-marker precedent). Round-3 delta re-check: the ESC-002 onOutput restructure does not touch this property (isRepaintChunk/isPureControl still classify the raw per-chunk input) - pre-existing baseline behavior, deferred not fixed.

claim: .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md:76-83 (baseline REQ-007) still asserts the retired tail-exclusion as CURRENT behavior ("a chunk that begins with a cursor-home sequence is treated as a screen repaint (isPureControl true) and excluded"), including the now-FALSE acceptance vector isPureControl(\x1b[Hreal text)===true — contradicting the shipped code (src/main/status/needs-input.ts:44), the amended CHAR-001 pin (TEST-2526), and the updated architecture.md bug #4 entry. REQ-011 (a MUST) requires retiring/amending every such claim across the whole docs tree plus CLAUDE.md and .orky/baseline/, and its stated CONV-023 grep pattern (cursor-home) matches inferred-spec.md:78 — yet the site was never amended. The 02-spec.md Baseline-supersession section names inferred-spec.md as the file holding the superseded clause but omits it from the REQ-011 site enumeration, and TEST-2532 five banned regexes (billed in 04-tests.md as the executable form of the CONV-023 grep) miss this phrasing, so the frozen doc-drift guard stays green while the stale present-tense claim survives.
fix: Amend baseline REQ-007 in .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md with a 0025 supersession note (pattern of the architecture.md bug #4 FIXED annotation): the tail-exclusion clause is superseded — a repaint chunk printable text IS admitted to the tail, quiet-timer exclusion preserved — and update the acceptance vector to isPureControl(\x1b[Hreal text)===false plus isRepaintChunk(...)===true. Also add isRepaintChunk to the needs-input.ts export inventory at .orky/baseline/architecture.md:58. Then extend the TEST-2532 BANNED list in tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts with the inferred-spec phrasing via the sanctioned post-freeze amendment path. propose CONV: A frozen doc-drift guard that claims to be the executable form of a spec retire-claims grep MUST ban a phrasing for every hit the spec grep pattern finds at tests-phase authoring time — an under-inclusive banned-phrasings list green-lights exactly the drift it exists to catch.
resolution: Verified fixed by the ESC-001 loopback, read directly from the artifacts: (1) .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md REQ-007 (lines 76-96) now carries a 0025 supersession blockquote quoting the retired tail-exclusion clause as PAST behavior, and its acceptance vector reads isPureControl of home+real-text === false plus isRepaintChunk === true; (2) .orky/baseline/architecture.md lines 58-59 list isRepaintChunk in the needs-input.ts export inventory and bug 4 reads KNOWN BUG - FIXED citing 0025; (3) tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts TEST-2532 BANNED list was extended (now 8 regexes) with both inferred-spec phrasings plus the pure-control-repaints vocabulary; witnessed green via npx vitest run tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts (6/6 pass, 2026-07-09). Re-ran the REQ-011 grep (cursor-home|cursor home|ESC-bracket-H|1;1H, case-insensitive) over docs/, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/ - every remaining hit is past-tense/supersession context, none asserts tail-exclusion as current.

claim: traceability.md Tests column still holds the plan-phase "(phase 4)" placeholders and its own footnote promises "tests columns are intentionally empty — phase 4 (tests) fills them in", but phase 4 filled only traceability.json (TEST-2501..TEST-2541); the human-readable matrix now silently lags the canonical json, leaving REQ->TEST traceability invisible to a reader of the .md artifact.
fix: Regenerate the Tests column of traceability.md from traceability.json, or replace the placeholder footnote with an explicit pointer that traceability.json is the canonical tests-phase-filled record.
resolution: Verified fixed: traceability.md Tests column is now filled for all 11 REQs and matches traceability.json tests arrays exactly (cross-checked entry by entry, TEST-2501..TEST-2541); the phase-4 placeholder footnote is replaced with a pointer naming traceability.json as the canonical tests-phase-filled record and instructing regeneration from it. (A residual Files-column divergence between the two files is a distinct defect, filed separately - not this finding.)

claim: The 0025 doc edit to the "Status tail must be ANSI-stripped" gotcha says the repaint exclusion is "per the next bullet", but the next bullet (CLAUDE.md:136) is "Avoid redundant PTY resizes"; the intended referent — the marker-less repaint-inert bullet — sits four bullets below at CLAUDE.md:167, so the cross-reference points a reader at the wrong rule.
fix: Reword the cross-reference to name its target instead of relying on adjacency, e.g. "per the marker-less-pane bullet below". (The parallel edit in docs/features/status-engine.md:74 says "see the next bullet" and is correct there — only CLAUDE.md needs the fix.)
resolution: Verified fixed: the CLAUDE.md Status-tail bullet (lines 130-136) now reads 'per the marker-less-pane bullet below' - the cross-reference names its target instead of relying on adjacency, and the marker-less-pane bullet at line 168 is the correct referent. Read directly from CLAUDE.md working copy.

claim: REQ-011's mandated scope (.orky/baseline/) still carries a present-tense claim of the retired behavior: inferred-spec.md's baseline REQ-007 entry ('Status tail is ANSI-stripped and screen-repaints are excluded') states 'a chunk that begins with a cursor-home sequence is treated as a screen repaint (isPureControl true) and excluded' and pins the acceptance isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text')===true — now FALSE against the shipped classifier (TEST-2526 pins false). The feature spec's own Baseline-supersession section says it intentionally amends baseline REQ-007, yet the baseline document itself was never amended. The REQ-011 grep ((?i)cursor-home|cursor home|...) run over the mandated scope hits this file (line 78), but plan TASK-007's file list omitted inferred-spec.md and the frozen doc-drift guard TEST-2532's BANNED regex list — billed in 04-tests.md as 'the executable form of the CONV-023 grep' — contains no regex that matches this phrasing, so nothing forces the retirement at doc-sync. Since CLAUDE.md says new Orky features are built against this baseline, a future feature reading baseline REQ-007's acceptance would encode a classification the code falsifies.
fix: At doc-sync (REQ-011's acceptance deadline), amend .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md REQ-007: mark the tail-exclusion clause superseded by 0025 (mirror architecture.md bug #4's FIXED pattern) and correct the acceptance line to the new classification (isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text')===false; repaint axis via isRepaintChunk); then re-run the REQ-011 grep over docs/, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/ and confirm zero present-tense hits. propose CONV: A frozen doc-drift guard described as the executable form of a spec's grep mandate MUST reproduce that mandate's full scope and pattern (or explicitly name each excluded hit); a hand-narrowed regex list over pre-enumerated files is not the executable form of the grep.
resolution: Duplicate of FINDING-004 (doc-drift), filed concurrently in the parallel review fan-out before the sibling's entry was visible: identical defect, identical location (.orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md:76-83), same root cause (TASK-007/TEST-2532 blind spot), and FINDING-004 carries the stronger fix (incl. architecture.md:58 export inventory + the sanctioned TEST-2532 amendment) plus an equivalent CONV proposal. Consolidating on FINDING-004 so the defect is tracked exactly once; the underlying defect remains OPEN there and still blocks (HIGH, contract-violation).

claim: The retired classification survives as stale mechanism VOCABULARY in living surfaces the REQ-011 grep pattern structurally cannot find (CONV-008 demands every phrasing, but '(?i)cursor-home|cursor home|\x1b\[H|1;1H' only keys on the home-sequence tokens): docs/features/ai-session-awareness.md still says the AGENT_WORKING_RE scan reads 'pure-control screen repaints' / '(pure-control repaints)' (lines 57, 69), and the byte-frozen pre-existing pins in tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts explain their own load-bearing mechanism with now-false comments — 'it is isPureControl, so it never touched lastOutputAt' (oscillation pin, ~94-95), 'redraws its working bar via cursor-home screen repaints, which are pure-control' (~125-126), and 'starts with cursor-home -> pure control' (~130). Post-0025 a repaint chunk carrying printable text is NOT isPureControl (needs-input.ts:44, TEST-2524/2526 pin false); the quiet-timer exemption rides isRepaintChunk. The described BEHAVIOR is still correct, but a maintainer debugging the ssh oscillation guard or the AI working-indicator path reads a mechanism explanation the shipped classifier falsifies.
fix: At doc-sync, rephrase ai-session-awareness.md lines 57/69 to the new vocabulary ('repaint chunks the quiet timer ignores' / 'home-prefixed repaint chunks', not 'pure-control repaints'). For the frozen test comments (comment-only, zero assertion change): record as a follow-up ledger entry to amend via the sanctioned post-freeze path, since the tests phase froze those files and REQ-003/REQ-008 required the pins byte-unchanged through this feature's gates.
resolution: Verified fixed in the ESC-001 loopback pass by reading every named site: docs/features/ai-session-awareness.md:57 now reads 'incl. cursor-home screen-repaint chunks, isRepaintChunk, which since feature 0025 feed the tail but still not the quiet timer' and :69 reads 'repaint chunks, which never feed the quiet timer' — the stale 'pure-control repaints' vocabulary is gone; the frozen tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts comments were amended (comment-only) through the sanctioned tests loopback rather than a follow-up entry (lines 94, 126, 132 now use repaint-chunk/isRepaintChunk vocabulary); and TEST-2532's BANNED list gained /pure-control (?:screen )?repaints/ so the fix is guarded on every future run (docs-feature-0025.test.ts green, 6/6).

claim: The status-engine feature doc's Testing enumeration was not brought current with 0025: it still maps the repaint behavior only to the pre-existing pins ('screen-redraw chunks not resetting quiet', 'a screen repaint never resurrects busy') and never mentions the suites that now pin the NEW behavior — the 'StatusTracker: repaint tail admission (0025)' describe (TEST-2501..2516) in tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts, tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts (the REQ-009 total-classifier contract), or the docs-feature-0025/regression-net-0025 guards. The doc's Behaviors bullets (74-75) and Key-files row (60, listing isRepaintChunk) WERE updated, so a developer reading the doc to locate where repaint tail admission is pinned finds the behavior described but not its tests — the Testing section is the doc's map to the pins and is now the only stale part of the page. Plan TASK-007 scoped the status-engine.md edit to the two Behaviors bullets only.
fix: At doc-sync, extend the status-engine.md Testing bullet for tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts with the 0025 repaint-tail-admission describe, and add one line for tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts (isPureControl/isRepaintChunk classifier contract, REQ-009).
resolution: Verified fixed by reading docs/features/status-engine.md Testing section (lines 102-104): the tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts bullet now names the 'StatusTracker: repaint tail admission (0025)' describe (TEST-2501..2516) and what it pins (tail admission, eviction re-closed, quiet-timer/state inertness); a new bullet covers tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts (the REQ-009 total-classifier contract for the isPureControl/isRepaintChunk split); and a further bullet names tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts + tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts. The doc's map-to-the-pins is current.

claim: REQ-011 MUST violation: baseline REQ-007 in .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md — the exact contract 02-spec.md declares this feature 'intentionally amends' — still asserts tail-exclusion as CURRENT behavior ('a chunk that begins with a cursor-home sequence is treated as a screen repaint (isPureControl true) and excluded') and pins the now-FALSE acceptance vector isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text')===true (the shipped code returns false; CHAR-001 was amended to pin exactly that). REQ-011 scopes the retirement MUST over the WHOLE .orky/baseline/ and fixes the grep (?i)cursor-home|cursor home|\x1b\[H|1;1H, which matches this file (line 78 'cursor-home'); yet TASK-007 (plan) enumerated only architecture.md under .orky/baseline and TEST-2532's five banned regexes (tests) cannot match this file's phrasing ('excluded, so a full-screen ConPTY repaint...' does not contain 'excluded from the status tail'), so the executable doc-drift guard is green over a stale, contradicting baseline contract — the CONV-008 failure mode ('never only the files a spec enumerated') encoded into the guard itself. A future spec-writer reading baseline REQ-007 encodes the retired contract.
fix: Amend .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md REQ-007 with a supersession note (feature 0025: repaint printable text IS admitted to the tail; quiet-timer/state exclusion only) and correct its acceptance vector to isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text')===false / isRepaintChunk true, matching the architecture.md bug-4 FIXED entry; extend TEST-2532's banned list (via the sanctioned tests-phase amendment path, since the suite is frozen) with a regex that pins this phrasing, and re-run the spec's own CONV-023 grep over the full scope (docs/, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/) before the gate closes, striking every remaining present-tense hit.
resolution: Duplicate consolidation, not a fix: identical defect to FINDING-004 (doc-drift), filed concurrently in the parallel fan-out before the sibling's entry was visible — same location (.orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md:76-83), same root cause (TASK-007 plan enumeration + TEST-2532 banned-list blind spot), both HIGH + contract-violation. Following the FINDING-007 precedent: consolidating on FINDING-004 so the defect is tracked exactly once; the underlying defect remains OPEN and blocking there. Independently verified by devils-advocate: the file's line 78 matches the spec's own CONV-023 grep and its acceptance vector isPureControl('\x1b[Hreal text')===true is falsified by the shipped code.

claim: The core scope claim of concept D1 / the spec — that full tail admission changes 'needs-input reachability ONLY' — is false, because the tail is shared state also consumed by computeIdleFallback (looksLikePrompt + the tailMatchesInputPrompt pre-empt). Verified against the built tracker with the production config (quietMs 10000, hard 5000): a marker-driven BUSY pane (onMarker C) with real output 'Compiling modules...' fed ONE home-prefixed resize repaint whose painted bottom line ends '%' (a progress footer) or '> ' flips busy->IDLE at tick(t0+5000+e) — 5s of real-output silence — while the command still runs; the pre-0025 control (repaint fully inert) stays BUSY. The spec's accepted-false-positive analysis (Design notes) names ONLY the needs-input flip; no REQ or test vector covers the idle-side leak (TEST-2504 uses a marker-LESS pane and a NON-prompt-shaped frame), and the fresh 0025 prose in docs/decisions.md asserts 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed' — demonstrably false. Consequence: a running marker-driven command can newly show idle (missed busy indicator) from a repaint that never touched the quiet timer, a state change that pre-0025 was impossible from any repaint.
fix: Decide and encode the idle-side consequence explicitly: EITHER keep a real-output-only view of the tail for computeIdleFallback's looksLikePrompt fast/hard paths (scoping admission to the needs-input decision the concept intended), OR accept the idle-side flip as chosen behavior with a spec REQ + unit vectors (marker-driven busy pane, repaint painting a looksLikePrompt-shaped bottom line, both the flip case and the pre-empt case) mirroring REQ-007's characterization pattern; in both cases correct docs/decisions.md's 'Only needs-input reachability changed' overclaim. propose CONV: A spec that admits new data into a shared buffer/state MUST enumerate the behavior change for EVERY decision path consuming that state, not only the consumer the feature targets — an accepted-consequences analysis scoped to the target consumer is incomplete by construction.
location: src/main/status/status-tracker.ts:65 (tail admission) + src/main/status/needs-input.ts:107-119 (computeIdleFallback); 02-spec.md Design notes; docs/decisions.md 0025 update
resolution: Deferred to docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md per explicit human decision recorded in ESC-001 (2026-07-09: defer FINDING-011/012 with doc disclosure), following the 0024 deferral-resolution precedent. Disclosure verified PRESENT by reading the artifacts: status-engine.md Behaviors carries the idle-side consequence as disclosed item (a) of 'Two disclosed consequences of repaint tail admission (0025)' and the followups ledger records it under MEDIUM with the revisit path (gate looksLikePrompt on last-contributor-is-real-output, or narrow admission). The underlying leak itself is deferred, not fixed (tail still shared with computeIdleFallback — status-tracker.ts:65, needs-input.ts:107-119). Residual defects in the disclosure's execution — the false 'self-heal on first real-output chunk' recovery claim for the marker-driven case, and decisions.md:749's uncorrected 'Only needs-input reachability changed' overclaim — are split out and tracked as FINDING-017 (open, MEDIUM).

claim: The needs-input state this feature makes newly reachable has NO exit path exercisable by its own target class, and the spec never discloses the resulting stuck state. The motivating program class — a full-screen TUI whose every frame is home-prefixed — runs raw-mode/no-echo, so the user's answer keystroke produces NO real output, and the TUI's resumed work arrives only as repaint chunks that REQ-003(b) (concept OQ7) forbids from resetting needs-input. Verified against the built tracker: after the repaint-delivered prompt fires needs-input (the fix working), 60 seconds of active, content-CHANGING home-prefixed work frames leave state 'needs-input' — the pane chip and the tab 'needs input' badge assert 'waiting for you' the whole time the program is demonstrably working, self-healing only on the first non-home-prefixed chunk or program exit. Pre-0025 this class could not enter needs-input at all (echo-on cooked-mode prompts, the previously reachable class, clear via the answer's echo — real output), so the exit gap is created BY this feature for exactly the class it serves. Concept D1's accepted-consequences list ('a repaint is not evidence the program resumed') records the mechanism but not this consequence: for this class, no admissible resume evidence can EVER exist under the current inputs — the user's own pty input (register-pty ptyWrite -> PtyManager.write), the true resume signal, is never consulted by StatusEngine/StatusTracker.
fix: Either (a) add the input-side reset — user data written to the pty while state is 'needs-input' resets to busy (a new tracker input fed from the pty write path; preserves locked OQ7 verbatim since a repaint still never resets, and cannot regress TEST-2510's resize-while-waiting hold because a resize writes no user data) — with unit vectors for answered-then-repainting TUIs; or (b) explicitly disclose and pin the stuck-needs-input consequence as chosen behavior (spec accepted-consequences + a characterization test naming it, REQ-007 pattern) so the trade is recorded rather than latent. propose CONV: A feature that makes a user-facing state newly reachable for an input class MUST verify an exit path exercisable by that same class's observable behavior, or disclose the stuck-state consequence in its accepted-consequences analysis.
resolution: Deferred to docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md per explicit human decision recorded in ESC-001 (2026-07-09: defer FINDING-011/012 with doc disclosure), following the 0024 deferral-resolution precedent. Disclosure verified PRESENT and ACCURATE by reading code + artifacts: status-engine.md Behaviors discloses it as item (b) of 'Two disclosed consequences of repaint tail admission (0025)' (raw-mode home-prefixed TUI: no exit path until the first non-repaint printable chunk or exit) and the followups ledger records it under MEDIUM with the rationale (false 'needs you' strictly better than the old wedged-busy; OQ4 content-delta named as the future fix). Verified the self-heal condition it claims is real: status-tracker.ts:71 resets needs-input->busy on printable non-repaint output, so the disclosed healing path exists for this consequence (unlike FINDING-011's, whose inaccurate recovery claim is tracked separately as FINDING-017). Deferral executed, not a code fix — the stuck-state itself remains as disclosed chosen behavior.

claim: StatusTracker.onOutput's new two-axis branching (printable/repaint) computes stripAnsi(text) redundantly and re-tests a condition it already knows: stripAnsi(text) now runs up to 3x per call for a repaint-with-printable-text chunk (once inside scanBuf's stripAnsi, once inside isPureControl's internal stripAnsi call, once again in the tail assignment stripAnsi(text)) where pre-0025 the same chunk short-circuited isPureControl via CURSOR_HOME_RE before any stripAnsi cost, and the second sequential 'if (printable && !repaint)' re-tests the already-true 'printable' from the prior 'if (printable)' block instead of nesting inside it.
fix: Compute 'const stripped = stripAnsi(text)' once and reuse it for the printable check (stripped.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f\s]/g,'').length===0) and the tail assignment; nest the real-output block inside 'if (printable) { ...; if (!repaint) {...} }' instead of two sequential top-level ifs, to remove both the redundant computation and the redundant boolean re-test in the method this feature just restructured.
resolution: Verified fixed by reading src/main/status/status-tracker.ts:40-86 (current working tree, part of commit 942609a). stripAnsi(text) is now computed exactly once explicitly (line 48, 'const stripped = stripAnsi(text)') and that single result is reused for both scanBuf (line 49) and the tail assignment (line 68) -- the redundant second and third explicit calls are gone. isPureControl(text) at line 63 still runs its own internal stripAnsi pass on the RAW chunk (not the precomputed 'stripped'), which is deliberate and documented in the comment at lines 60-62 ('Classification reads the RAW chunk ... handing it pre-stripped text would misjudge escapes formed by the stripping itself') -- this satisfies the finding's own judgment call ('judge whether that satisfies the finding's intent'): total physical stripAnsi call sites are now 2 (1 explicit + 1 necessarily-separate internal), down from 3, and the second is not redundant, it is correctness-required. The un-nested re-test is also fixed: the real-output block is now nested inside 'if (printable) { this.tail = ...; if (!isRepaintChunk(text)) { ... } }' (lines 64-84) instead of two sequential top-level ifs. Confirmed green: npx vitest run tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts tests/main/needs-input.test.ts tests/characterization-status.test.ts tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts -- 6 files / 91 tests passed.

claim: features/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression/traceability.md claims to mirror the canonical traceability.json ('regenerate this table from it rather than editing by hand') but does not: its Files column is missing tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts (REQ-008), tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts and tests/main/needs-input.test.ts (REQ-009/REQ-010), and tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts (REQ-011) that ARE present in traceability.json's REQ file arrays -- while conversely REQ-011's .md row was hand-edited to add .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md (documented as an ESC-001 correction) that traceability.json's REQ-011.files array was never updated to include. The doc's own stated single-source-of-truth relationship is false in both directions.
fix: Regenerate traceability.md's Files column programmatically from traceability.json (or hand-sync both directions once): add .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md to traceability.json's REQ-011.files, and add the four missing files above to traceability.md's REQ-008/009/010/011 rows, so the 'mirrors traceability.json' claim is actually true.
resolution: Verified fixed by reading both artifacts side by side. traceability.json REQ-011.files now includes '.orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md' (was missing before, per the fix). traceability.md's Files column now includes, for every REQ, exactly the same file list as traceability.json: REQ-008 gained tests/regression-net-0025.test.ts, REQ-009 gained tests/main/needs-input-classifier.test.ts + tests/main/needs-input.test.ts, REQ-010 already listed both, and REQ-011's row now lists all 8 files incl. .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md and tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts -- cross-checked entry by entry across all 11 REQs, zero divergence in either direction. The .md footnote no longer claims a false single-source-of-truth relationship: it now reads 'This table is REGENERATED from traceability.json (the canonical record) -- regenerate rather than hand-edit (FINDING-014)' plus a second line naming the ESC-001/ESC-002 provenance of the inferred-spec.md addition to both files. All three verification points from the task are confirmed: inferred-spec.md is in json REQ-011.files, the md Files column is complete and matches json, and the footnote states the regeneration relationship.

claim: One stale-mechanism comment of the FINDING-008 class survives in the PRODUCTION file itself, missed by the loopback's vocabulary sweep: StatusTracker.onOutput's opening comment still says the AGENT_WORKING_RE scan reads 'ALL output (including screen-repaints classified as pure control below)'. Post-0025 that parenthetical is false for exactly the load-bearing case the sentence is about — a working-indicator repaint by definition carries printable text ('esc to interrupt'), so it is isRepaintChunk true / isPureControl FALSE, and there is no 'classified as pure control below' for it. The same loopback rewrote every other comment in this method (the 0025 block at lines 54-58, the LOCKED-decision block at 72-79) and amended the equivalent falsified comments in tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts and docs/features/ai-session-awareness.md, but the method's own header comment — the first thing a maintainer reads when debugging the AI working-indicator path — was left asserting the retired classification. REQ-011's grep scope (docs/, CLAUDE.md, .orky/baseline/) and TEST-2532's .md-only walk structurally cannot catch source comments, so nothing guards this site.
fix: Reword the parenthetical to the new two-axis vocabulary, e.g. 'Scan ALL output (including repaint chunks, isRepaintChunk, whose text is admitted to the tail below but which never touch the quiet timer or state) for the AI agent's working indicator' — comment-only, zero behavior change, can ride the doc-sync pass.
resolution: Verified fixed by reading src/main/status/status-tracker.ts:41-44: StatusTracker.onOutput's header comment no longer says 'screen-repaints classified as pure control below' — it now reads 'including repaint chunks, `isRepaintChunk` — whose printable text is admitted to the tail below but which never touch the quiet timer or state', the exact two-axis vocabulary the finding's fix prescribed, and it matches the shipped classification (isRepaintChunk-gated quiet-timer/state exemption at lines 63-83). Comment-only change; assertions/behavior untouched.

claim: NOT a re-file of FINDING-011 (the deferred idle-side behavior itself) — this is a residual of the ESC-001 disclosure pass's execution: the pass added status-engine.md's disclosure that the shared tail lets a repaint-painted prompt-shaped last line flip a busy marker-driven pane to IDLE (consequence (a)), yet the same pass left docs/decisions.md's fresh 0025 update block ending with 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed.' — the exact sentence FINDING-011 verified false against the built tracker. Two living docs now directly contradict each other on what 0025 changed, and decisions.md is the surface CLAUDE.md sends every maintainer/agent to for the *why*. Worse, the followups ledger's FINDING-011 entry records only 'disclosed in status-engine.md' and omits the decisions.md correction that FINDING-011's own fix text names, so when FINDING-011 is eventually closed as deferred-per-human-decision, no artifact tracks the surviving falsehood.
fix: One-sentence doc edit alongside the disclosure (no reopening of the deferral): replace 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed.' with wording consistent with the disclosure, e.g. 'The intended change is needs-input reachability; the shared tail also feeds computeIdleFallback, giving two disclosed residual consequences — see status-engine.md and the 0025 review follow-ups.' Also append the decisions.md correction to the followups ledger's FINDING-011 'If it bites' line so closing 011 cannot strand it.
location: docs/decisions.md:749 (0025 update block) vs docs/features/status-engine.md:76-86; docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md FINDING-011 entry
resolution: Verified both halves fixed by reading the artifacts. (1) docs/decisions.md 0025 update block (lines 749-755): the false 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed.' sentence is gone, replaced by 'The *intended* change is needs-input reachability; because the tail is shared state also read by computeIdleFallback, two residual consequences were disclosed and deferred (...)' naming BOTH consequences — incl. the FINDING-017-corrected 'for the command's remainder' recovery for the idle-side flip — with links to status-engine.md → Behaviors and the 0025 review follow-ups; no living-doc contradiction remains between decisions.md and status-engine.md's disclosure. (2) The followups ledger's FINDING-011 entry (docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md:33-35) now records the decisions.md correction explicitly ('the decisions.md 0025 update block's "only needs-input reachability changed" overclaim was corrected in the same pass (FINDING-016)'), so closing 011 cannot strand the fix.

location: docs/features/status-engine.md:85 ('Both self-heal on the first real-output chunk'); docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md:28 ('the next real output re-busies'); docs/decisions.md:749 ('Only needs-input *reachability* changed')
claim: The FINDING-011 deferral disclosure (written in the ESC-001 loopback) misstates the deferred consequence's recovery, understating the risk the human accepted: both disclosure sites claim the spurious busy->idle flip self-heals on the next real output, but both sites describe a marker-DRIVEN pane, and for a marker-driven pane real output NEVER re-busies an idle tracker — StatusTracker.onOutput gates busy-marking on !this.hasMarkers (src/main/status/status-tracker.ts:80) and tick() transitions only FROM 'busy' — so a pane spuriously idled by a repaint-painted prompt-shaped line stays idle for the ENTIRE remainder of the running command, recovering only at the next command-start marker (B/C). The self-heal claim is true only for marker-LESS panes, which neither disclosure site describes. The recorded risk acceptance ('defer + disclose', ESC-001) is thus documented against a transient consequence when the actual consequence is command-lifetime loss of the busy indicator. Additionally, docs/decisions.md:749's 0025 update paragraph still asserts 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed' — the exact overclaim FINDING-011's fix asked to correct — which now directly contradicts status-engine.md's own disclosure (a) (a busy->idle reachability change is disclosed two files away). Verified against the shipped state machine, not the docs.
fix: Correct both disclosure sites to state the real recovery for the marker-driven case: the spurious idle persists until the next command-start marker (i.e. for the current command's remaining lifetime); only a marker-less pane re-busies on the next real output. Replace docs/decisions.md:749's 'Only needs-input *reachability* changed' with wording that acknowledges the disclosed idle-side consequence (e.g. 'needs-input reachability changed; one disclosed idle-side consequence of the shared tail is recorded in status-engine.md / the 0025 follow-ups'). While editing, also tighten the (b) healing phrase 'first non-home-prefixed chunk' to 'first printable non-repaint chunk' (a non-home pure-control chunk like \x1b[2J heals nothing either). propose CONV: A disclosure recording a human-approved risk deferral MUST have its behavioral claims (recovery path, self-heal conditions, duration) verified against the actual state machine or code, exactly like spec acceptance text — an understated disclosure corrupts the recorded acceptance it exists to document.
resolution: Verified fixed at all three disclosure sites by reading the artifacts against the shipped state machine: (1) status-engine.md Behaviors disclosure (a) now states 'Recovery is NOT next-output for a marker-driven pane: the busy rule is gated on !hasMarkers and tick() transitions only from busy, so the pane stays idle for the command's remainder and recovers at the next command-start marker (B/C); only a marker-LESS pane re-busies on the next real output' — the command-lifetime recovery + marker-less distinction, verified accurate against status-tracker.ts (the !hasMarkers gate, tick() busy-only transitions); (2) the followups ledger's FINDING-011 entry carries the same corrected recovery ('command-lifetime on a marker-driven pane... only a marker-less pane re-busies'), the FINDING-017 attribution, the REAFFIRMED 2026-07-09 human decision, and now names the decisions.md correction so closing 011 cannot strand it; (3) docs/decisions.md's 0025 update block no longer says 'Only needs-input reachability changed' — it reads 'The intended change is needs-input reachability; ...two residual consequences were disclosed and deferred (...idle a busy marker-driven pane for the command's remainder...)' with cross-references to both detailed sites. Residual: the secondary 'while editing, also tighten' item ((b)'s 'first non-home-prefixed chunk' healing phrase) was applied only in decisions.md ('next real-output chunk') — split out as a new LOW, not grounds to hold this MEDIUM open.

claim: CONV-018 violation: the ESC-001 loopback comment-amended two frozen regression pins in tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts - the oscillation pin (a screen repaint does NOT resurrect busy on a marker-less pane, lines 90-96) and the AI-exception pin (keeps an AI session BUSY while it shows esc to interrupt, lines 123-129); verified via git diff HEAD: vocabulary-comment hunks only, assertions identical - but the arbitration was never propagated into the frozen artifacts. 02-spec.md REQ-008 still says these nets MUST pass without modification and REQ-003 says the pins pass unmodified (the spec-freeze token reads spec unchanged since the spec gate); 04-tests.md line 11 still claims ALL pre-existing tests byte-unchanged, incl. the two REQ-003/REQ-008 frozen pins, and lines 51-55 claim all byte-unchanged and green; and the new 0025 describe header the tests phase itself added (status-tracker.test.ts ~line 160) still states the two pins MUST stay unmodified while one of them now differs from HEAD in the same file. The frozen spec and the phase-4 artifact therefore contradict the shipped test file - exactly the state CONV-018 forbids (an arbitration recorded only in state.json). Note FINDING-008 own fix text prescribed recording the test-comment amendments as a FOLLOW-UP precisely because REQ-003/REQ-008 required the pins byte-unchanged through this feature gates; the loopback amended them in-feature anyway without amending the requirements or the artifact.
fix: Propagate ESC-001 into the artifacts through the sanctioned loopback path (CONV-018: normative text + acceptance in the same loopback): amend 02-spec.md REQ-003/REQ-008 acceptance to say assertions/behavior unmodified, with the ESC-001-sanctioned comment-only vocabulary amendment named explicitly; correct 04-tests.md line 11 and lines 51-55 (byte-unchanged -> assertions-byte-unchanged, enumerating the two comment hunks the way the TEST-2527 collision record does); and reword the in-file describe header MUST-stay-unmodified sentence to name the sanctioned amendment. Alternative: revert the two comment hunks to restore byte-parity with HEAD and record the FINDING-008 test-comment half as the follow-up ledger entry its own fix text prescribed.
resolution: ESC-002 propagated the ESC-001 arbitration through the sanctioned review->spec loopback; verified directly from the artifacts, not the claim: (1) 02-spec.md REQ-003/REQ-008 acceptance now reads 'assertion bodies unmodified' and names the ESC-001 comment-only vocabulary amendments explicitly (isPureControl -> isRepaintChunk phrasing, git diff shows comment hunks only); (2) 04-tests.md line 11 corrected to assertion-body-unchanged, the lines 51-56 claims corrected, and a new 'ESC-001/ESC-002 amendment record (CONV-018)' section enumerates both amendments in the TEST-2527 record pattern; (3) the in-file describe header (tests/main/status-tracker.test.ts:163-166) now names the sanctioned amendment and cites 02-spec.md REQ-003/REQ-008 + 04-tests.md; (4) ground truth re-verified: git diff 9192411..HEAD on status-tracker.test.ts shows exactly two comment-only hunks in the pre-existing region (oscillation pin + working-bar pin incl. its inline vector comment) with every assertion body byte-identical, and git diff --stat is EMPTY for marker-less-pane.spec.ts / fake-remote-shell.mjs / status.spec.ts / pane-status-css.test.ts (REQ-008 byte-unchanged set); (5) the propagation rode the sanctioned path: state.json records ESC-002 + the loopback, spec/plan/tests/implement gates re-passed 2026-07-10T02:07-02:14, spec-freeze.json hash equals sha256 of the amended 02-spec.md and test-freeze.json equals sha256 of the current status-tracker.test.ts. Spec, phase-4 artifact, in-file header, and shipped test file are now coherent.

claim: 04-tests.md TEST-2532 row still describes the frozen doc-drift guard as (the five enumerated regexes, all live today - the executable form of the CONV-023 grep), but tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts BANNED list now holds EIGHT regexes: the ESC-001 loopback added the two inferred-spec.md phrasings (treated as a screen repaint (isPureControl true); the retired ===true acceptance vector) and the ai-session-awareness pure-control-repaints vocabulary (FINDING-008). The phase-4 artifact records the ESC-001 tests-phase amendment nowhere (zero hits for ESC-001 / FINDING-004 / inferred-spec / loopback in the file), so the frozen tests record no longer describes the frozen suite it exists to document - a reader auditing the guard against the artifact sees an unexplained three-regex discrepancy. Distinct from FINDING-018 (the byte-unchanged-pins claims at lines 11/51-55); same root cause: the loopback amended frozen tests without updating the artifact that records them.
fix: Update the TEST-2532 row to the eight-regex reality and add a short ESC-001 amendment note in 04-tests.md (the same pattern as the TEST-2527 CONV-059 collision record already in the file). propose CONV: A loopback that amends frozen tests or extends a REQ file scope MUST update, in the same loopback, every phase artifact that records the frozen state (the tests-phase artifact test descriptions and status column, traceability.json AND its human-readable mirror) - a loopback that edits only the frozen files leaves the phase record describing a suite that no longer exists.
resolution: Verified fixed: the 04-tests.md TEST-2532 row now states the eight-regex reality ('EIGHT enumerated regexes: the original five ... plus three added by the ESC-001 loopback', naming the two .orky/baseline/inferred-spec.md phrasings per FINDING-004 and the pure-control-repaints vocabulary per FINDING-008), and the new 'ESC-001/ESC-002 amendment record (CONV-018)' section documents the 5->8 banned-list extension in exactly the TEST-2527 collision-record pattern the fix prescribed. Cross-checked against tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts: the BANNED array holds exactly 8 regexes matching the description one for one; the guard was witnessed green in this re-review (npx vitest run tests/docs-feature-0025.test.ts, 6/6 pass, 2026-07-09). The phase-4 artifact again describes the frozen suite it records.

claim: ANSI_RE's OSC-terminator alternatives (\][^\x07]*\x07 and \][^\x1b]*\x1b\) are unreachable dead code: the FIRST alternative's char class [@-Z\-_] is parsed as ranges @-Z (0x40-0x5A) plus \-_ (0x5C-0x5F), and 0x5D is ']' -- so ESC+']' (the OSC introducer) always matches the first, single-character alternative before the OSC-specific alternatives are ever tried (JS regex alternation stops at the first alternative that lets the overall match succeed, and nothing follows the group). Verified empirically: stripAnsi('\x1b]0;password:\x07REST') === '0;password:\x07REST' -- only the 2-byte ESC+']' introducer is removed; the entire OSC payload (window-title text, OSC 8 hyperlink URI, OSC 52 clipboard data, etc., up to its BEL/ST terminator) survives as literal 'printable' text. status-engine.ts:62 feeds StatusTracker.onOutput raw, untrusted PTY bytes with zero prior sanitization, and onOutput now (0025) admits a CURSOR_HOME_RE-prefixed (repaint) chunk's printable text to the needs-input tail whenever isPureControl(text) is false -- pre-0025 any home-prefixed chunk was ALWAYS excluded from the tail outright, regardless of trailing content, so this specific admission path (repaint prefix + trailing unstripped OSC payload) is newly reachable under 0025's widened tail-admission rule. The same unstripped OSC payload also reaches scanBuf (AGENT_WORKING_RE) on every chunk, repaint or not. Net effect: any program or remote host producing PTY output (ssh session, motd, a script run in-pane) can smuggle attacker-chosen text past stripAnsi by wrapping it in an OSC sequence, e.g. ESC ] 0 ; 'Enter password: ' BEL, poisoning the needs-input tail / AI-working scanBuf that drive the pane's busy/idle/needs-input classification and OS notifications -- content that a correct terminal never renders on screen at all.
fix: Exclude ']' (0x5D) from the first ANSI_RE alternative so the OSC introducer falls through to the dedicated OSC alternatives, e.g. change [@-Z\-_] to [@-Z\^_] (list \, ^, _ individually instead of a \-_ range that accidentally spans ']'). Add characterization vectors to needs-input.ts's stripAnsi tests: an OSC 0/1/2 title sequence (BEL-terminated) and an OSC 8 hyperlink (ST-terminated, ESC\) must be stripped IN FULL, not just their 2-byte introducer -- assert stripAnsi('\x1b]0;x\x07REST')==='REST'. Once fixed, re-verify the repaint-tail-admission tests (TEST-2501..2516) still pass with an OSC payload appended after a CURSOR_HOME_RE prefix.
location: src/main/status/needs-input.ts:23 (ANSI_RE definition); src/main/status/status-tracker.ts:48-49,63-68 (stripped text admitted to scanBuf/tail from raw PTY input, status-engine.ts:62)
resolution: deferred to its own feature per human decision 2026-07-09; recorded in the 0025 followups ledger + baseline architecture.md known-bugs item 5

claim: Residual of the ESC-002 disclosure correction, split out of FINDING-017 at its closure: the (b) consequence's healing condition is stated with two different vocabularies across the three disclosure sites, and the one used by status-engine.md and the followups ledger ('first non-home-prefixed chunk') overstates healing — i.e. understates the stuck-needs-input duration, the same error direction FINDING-017 corrected for (a). A non-home-prefixed chunk heals nothing unless it is printable non-repaint output: the needs-input->busy reset (status-tracker.ts:73) sits inside 'if (printable)' + 'if (!isRepaintChunk)', so a non-home PURE-CONTROL chunk (e.g. '\x1b[2J', or the '\x1b[?1049l' alt-buffer-leave a raw-mode TUI emits) is fully inert (isPureControl -> no tail, no timer, no state). docs/decisions.md:752 already carries the accurate form ('until the next real-output chunk' — 'real output' being the spec-defined printable non-repaint class), so the loopback applied FINDING-017's named tightening to one site of three, leaving the two detailed disclosure sites slightly looser than the summary site they anchor. Magnitude is small (a raw-mode TUI's non-home chunks are almost always printable), which is why this is LOW, not a re-open of the MEDIUM.
fix: One-phrase edit at both sites: replace 'the first non-home-prefixed chunk' with 'the first real-output (printable non-repaint) chunk', matching decisions.md's vocabulary and the spec's Vocabulary section. Comment/doc-only; can ride the next doc pass — no reopening of the FINDING-012 deferral.
location: docs/features/status-engine.md:87 ('no exit path until the first non-home-prefixed chunk or exit'); docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md:44 ('self-healing only on the first non-home-prefixed chunk or exit')
resolution: Fixed the two named consequence-(b) healing-phrase sites: docs/features/status-engine.md's disclosed-consequence bullet and the FINDING-012 entry in docs/superpowers/0025-cursor-home-output-suppression-review-followups.md now both say the exit path is 'the first real-output (printable, non-repaint) chunk or exit' instead of the inaccurate 'first non-home-prefixed chunk' (which wrongly implies a non-home pure-control chunk like \x1b[2J heals the stuck state). docs/decisions.md already used the accurate phrasing and was left untouched.
